featured.name: Falcon Perception
featured.tagline: 0.6B parameter early-fusion transformer for open-vocabulary grounding and segmentation from natural language prompts.
featured.why_radar: TII's latest model breaks the traditional vision encoder-decoder separation with early fusion architecture. At just 0.6B parameters, it makes sophisticated visual grounding accessible for edge deployment. This architectural shift towards unified vision-language processing could reshape how we build multimodal systems.

featured.name: Trinity Large Thinking
featured.tagline: Apache 2.0 open reasoning model for long-horizon agents and tool use.
featured.why_radar: Finally, a proper open-weight reasoning model that doesn't hide behind restrictive licenses. Arcee's Apache 2.0 release means developers can actually inspect, modify, and deploy reasoning capabilities without legal gymnastics. The focus on long-horizon agent workflows hits exactly where the industry needs transparency most.

picks.name: Gemma 4
picks.tagline: Google's most capable open model family with Apache 2.0 licensing for the first time.
picks.why_radar: Google finally drops the custom licence nonsense and goes full Apache 2.0. Four models spanning smartphone to workstation deployment means practical local AI without vendor lock-in. This licensing shift signals Google's serious play for the open-source developer mindshare.

picks.name: MAI-Transcribe-1
picks.tagline: Speech-to-text model running 2.5x faster than predecessor at $0.36 per audio hour.
picks.why_radar: Microsoft's pricing play hits the transcription market hard. 2.5x speed improvements with 25-language support plus noise handling makes this a proper Whisper competitor. The $0.36 per hour pricing shows Microsoft is serious about grabbing market share from OpenAI's transcription dominance.

picks.name: Sakana Marlin
picks.tagline: AI assistant that researches autonomously for up to eight hours for business strategy work.
picks.why_radar: Sakana's "Ultra Deep Research" positioning targets the consulting industry's bread and butter. Eight-hour autonomous research sessions suggest they're solving the context length and attention problems that plague current AI assistants. If it works, strategy consulting just got disrupted.

picks.name: MindsDB Anton
picks.tagline: Autonomous BI agent for business intelligence workflows.
picks.why_radar: MindsDB's BI agent tackles the perennial problem of making data accessible to non-technical users. Autonomous business intelligence could finally deliver on the promise of self-service analytics. The timing is right as organisations drown in data but struggle with insights.

hn_top5.title: Ask HN: What is your dev set up like?
hn_top5.summary: Community discussion about development environments and tooling setups. Developers sharing their preferred configurations, from local machines to cloud setups.

hn_top5.title: MindsDB Anton: a new powerful autonomous BI agent
hn_top5.summary: MindsDB launches Anton, an AI agent focused on business intelligence automation. Promises to make data analysis more accessible through autonomous query generation and insight discovery.

hn_top5.title: Show HN: Skales – I built a desktop AI agent a 6-year-old can use
hn_top5.summary: Developer creates Skales, a desktop AI agent designed for extreme simplicity and child-friendly interaction. Focuses on making AI accessible through intuitive interfaces rather than technical complexity.

hn_top5.title: Show HN: An MCP server for Devops automation
hn_top5.summary: New Model Context Protocol server targeting DevOps workflows and automation tasks. Integrates with existing tools to provide AI-assisted operations management.

hn_top5.title: Genesis Agent – A self-modifying AI agent that runs local
hn_top5.summary: Open-source AI agent built with Electron and Ollama that can modify its own behaviour locally. Represents the growing trend towards autonomous, self-improving AI systems.
